highlights.text: I provided targets at the close Tuesday, stating: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
Institutions buy dips in a very standardized way: They wait for price to put in a deep “elevator down” sell.
Case in point last Wednesday: Last Tuesday late day ES went “elevator down” 60 points down to 6680.

I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “Another option to long is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low at 6693.” Early Wednesday morning we flushed 6693 down to 6680, recovered 6693, then went parabolic to 6800 high last week.
Case in point again this Tuesday: ES went “elevator down” from 6800 down to 6747.
I wrote Monday at 4pm: “Th e far safer option is to wait for it to hold then put in a Failed Breakdown of the Friday afternoon 6754 low .” We got this around noon Tuesday, and longs triggered.
Throughout this, ES had been building a large flag mostly 6750 to 6800 with 6765 being a key magnet in the middle, and Tuesday’s Failed Breakdown took us to resistance yesterday.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into two quality Failed Breakdowns we had this week: One on Monday that gave us a 35 point leg up, and another on Tuesday that gave us a 60+ point leg up.

post_title: Will Today's Micro-Dip In SPX Get Bought Like Tuesday's? Oct 10th Plan
